//: Default modern blast light: three waves expand from the centre — golden-yellow (color_a), then red (color_b), then a clear wave that fades each tile out. It is a translucent light cover (never opaque): a lit tile's opacity decays from alpha_a (bright flash) to alpha_b (dimmer glow) as the light burns, staying brighter than the environment until the clear wave takes it to zero. Per-tile spread jitter makes the wavefronts irregular; in a big blast the wave_gap spacing makes the rings read as distinct travelling waves. Used by any explosion that does not name its own light_effect. Phase two adds a screen-space shockwave: a refraction ring that warps the rendered scene as it expands (CPU mesh warp at present time; silently skipped on backends without RenderGeometry).

Fierier, longer-lingering variant for incendiary blasts: deeper orange A, dark-red B, wider wave spacing and stronger flicker/jitter so the rings are more pronounced and the edge more ragged. Slightly more opaque than the default while still a translucent light cover. Stronger, slightly slower shockwave with a wider refraction band.

Seven-stop rainbow blast, a demo of the N-stop colour ramp: red -> orange -> yellow -> green -> blue -> indigo -> violet, then a clear wave. Each stop is one expanding front (wave_gap spacing pulls the seven rings apart so they read as distinct travelling bands); smoothstep easing softens every hue/alpha transition. The opacity keyframes ride high then taper, so the inner bands stay bright while the outer violet fades. Wider/longer than the default so all seven rings have room to separate; includes a screen-shake and a disc shockwave for the full effect. Preview it with the debug 'Preview explosion VFX' command.

Muzzle flash for gunfire. A single bright, near-instant point: hot white core flashing to muzzle-orange, gone in ~90ms. High flicker for a live, uneven spark; no shockwave; a tiny screen shake so heavy guns have a little kick. Triggered per shot from Character::fire_gun (suppressed for silenced / low-loudness / archery weapons and only when the muzzle tile is visible). Single tile, so duration_per_tile_ms barely matters; the floor keeps it snappy.

Bullet impact spark. A minimal white-to-grey point at the point of impact (any tile: creature, wall, ground, or a clean miss). Even faster and fainter than the muzzle flash, no screen shake. Played from projectile_attack at the trajectory end.

Faint bullet tracer. A very dim, brief warm-white line swept from muzzle to impact along the flight path, drawn under the per-tile bullet sprite for visible normal-bullet shots. Single stop, low alpha, quick fade, no shockwave or shake; meant to read as a streak, not a beam.

Laser beam (red). Bright crimson line from muzzle to impact, fast fade, with a directional shockwave so the beam visibly refracts the scene along its axis. Used for LASER ammo and the BULLETS_AS_LASERS option fallback.
Plasma beam (cyan-green). Hotter, slightly thicker energy bolt for PLASMA ammo. Brighter core and stronger refraction than the laser.
Lightning arc (white-blue). For LIGHTNING ammo: a stark blue-white bolt with strong flicker and a sharp, fast refraction snap.

EMP / electromagnetic detonation. Blue-white electric flash with strong flicker and no fiery embers: a quick rise, cool-toned stops, short duration, and a fast, sharp refraction ring. For EMP grenades and similar.

Flashbang. A single blinding white stop held near-opaque, longer than a blast and with a strong screen shake, then a clean fade. No colour ramp (just white) and no shockwave refraction — it is glare, not a pressure wave.
Gas / smoke release. A slow, low-opacity green-yellow haze with a long lifetime, soft flicker and no shockwave — reads as billowing vapour rather than a flash. For toxic gas, acid, and smoke clouds.

Enhanced incendiary blast. Deeper, longer-burning fire than fire_blast: orange -> deep red -> ember stops, slow waves, heavy flicker and a long tail, with a strong slow shockwave. For napalm / thermobaric / clinging-fire explosions.

Neutral smoke release (grey, not toxic green). Unlike the blast recipes this is NOT an outward-sweeping ring: wave_travel is near zero so the whole puff appears together, jitter (not radius) drives per-tile arrival for an irregular cloud edge, and it holds at low opacity then fades softly over a long life. Reads as billowing smoke settling in place rather than an explosion bloom. For smoke bombs / smoke mortars; distinct from gas_cloud (green-yellow toxic vapour).
